content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

The score of a permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N] is defined as follows:


If Pi=iP_i = iPi‚Äč=i for at least one iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the score is ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, the score is the number of indices iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N) such that APi‚Č§AiA_{P_i} \le A_iAPi‚Äč‚Äč‚Č§Ai‚Äč.
Find the maximum possible score for some permutation PPP. You also need to print a valid permutation.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output the following:



On a new line, first output the maximum possible score.
Next, output NNN integers, P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč, any valid permutation that obtains the maximum score.

If multiple answers are possible, all will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Any permutation PPP which does not have Pi=iP_i = iPi‚Äč=i for any index iii, would have a score of 333 because all values are 111, so APi‚Č§AiA_{P_i} \le A_iAPi‚Äč‚Äč‚Č§Ai‚Äč always holds.
Test Case 2 : P=[3,1,2]P = [3, 1, 2]P=[3,1,2] means that index i=1,3i = 1, 3i=1,3 are good because AP1=A3=2‚Č§A1A_{P_1} = A_3 = 2 \le A_1AP1‚Äč‚Äč=A3‚Äč=2‚Č§A1‚Äč, and AP3=A2=1‚Č§A3A_{P_3} = A_2 = 1 \le A_3AP3‚Äč‚Äč=A2‚Äč=1‚Č§A3‚Äč. It can be shown that a larger score is impossible.
